content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); vertex 111 is the root. Each vertex has a weight; let's denote the weight of vertex iii by wiw_iwi​.

You should answer QQQ queries. The queries have to be processed online, i.e. to obtain each query, you need the answer to the previous query.

In each query, you are given a vertex vvv and a parameter kkk. For each vertex uuu in the subtree of vvv (including vvv), consider the value wu⊕kw_u \oplus kwu​⊕k (⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation). The answer to this query is the maximum of these values and the smallest uuu such that vertex uuu is in the subtree of vertex vvv and wu⊕kw_u \oplus kwu​⊕k is equal to this maximum.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2,0002,0002,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers w1,w2,…,wNw_1, w_2, \ldots, w_Nw1​,w2​,…,wN​.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that there is an edge between nodes xxx and yyy.
The next QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb. The parameters vvv and kkk can be obtained in the following way: let's denote the value and vertex from the answer to the previous query by xlx_lxl​ and vlv_lvl​ respectively (xl=vl=0x_l = v_l = 0xl​=vl​=0 if this is the first query); then, v=a⊕vlv = a \oplus v_lv=a⊕vl​ and k=b⊕xlk = b \oplus x_lk=b⊕xl​.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing two space-separated integers — the answer to the query, i.e. the number of the vertex for which we get the maximum value and the maximum value.
